steps.title: Identify slope direction and magnitude
steps.content:
The scatterplot shows y decreasing as x increases, confirming a negative slope and eliminating positive-slope choices A and C.

From the leftmost point near $(0, 10)$ to the rightmost points near $(9.5, 2)$:
$$\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x} = \frac{2-10}{9.5-0} \approx -0.84$$
This gentle decline matches a slope of $-0.9$, not $-9.4$ (which would drop y by nearly 10 units per unit of x). Choice D is favored over B.

steps.title: Confirm the y-intercept
steps.content: At $x = 0$, the scatterplot shows a point at approximately $y = 10$. Choice D, $y = 9.4 - 0.9x$, has y-intercept $9.4$, matching the observed value. Choice B has y-intercept $0.9$, far too low. That matches choice D — D is correct.
